brand.name: Think+ (Think Energy Plus)
brand.vertical: Network marketing / clean energy retail (B2C & MLM advisor channel)

summary: Think+ is the network-marketing channel of Think Energy, a licensed retail clean-electricity provider. People join for $99/year as Energy Advisors and earn acquisition bonuses plus monthly residuals by referring residential and small-business customers to fixed-rate clean electricity and community-solar plans across 13 deregulated US states plus DC. It serves entrepreneurial, relationship-driven side-hustlers who want flexible income, alongside the end customers they refer for cleaner, price-certain energy.

campaigns.name: New Advisor Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive new Energy Advisors to their first 'aha' — enroll their own home and make their first customer referral within 7 days.
campaigns.trigger: Energy Advisor enrollment / $99 membership purchased (account created). Email 1 fires within seconds.
campaigns.segment: New Energy Advisors, days 0–90; branch on whether they have enrolled their own home yet.
campaigns.rationale: New advisors get no structured onboarding today and ~70–80% churn in year one; the fastest path to retention is getting them to their first bonus, which proves the model actually works.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations, and give one clear first action: enroll your own home to earn back the $99 and start the 7-day first-referral clock.
emails.subject: You're in, [First name] — here's how to earn your $99 back this month
emails.preview: Two minutes to switch your own home to clean energy — your first move as an Advisor.
emails.cta: Switch my home to clean energy
keyPoints: Single first action: open your Web Office and enroll your own home — the offset on your own bill is the fastest proof the model works
Set the goal plainly: enroll your home today, make your first customer referral within 7 days
Human, peer-to-peer voice — set expectations for a short onboarding series and invite a real reply for help
Include FTC typical-results disclosure on any reference to earning the $99 back; no income guarantees

campaigns.name: Opportunity Conversion (Webinar & Info-Call → Enrolled Advisor)
campaigns.objective: Convert opportunity-curious prospects (webinar attendees, info-call takers) into enrolled Energy Advisors using the near-zero-risk 'earn back your $99' entry.
campaigns.trigger: Webinar attended / info-call completed / 'learn more' requested — with a short follow-up urgency window running in parallel.
campaigns.segment: Prospects who engaged with the opportunity but have not enrolled; branch by warm (attended live) vs. passive (registered/no-show).
campaigns.rationale: The sales cycle is same-day self-serve, so a timely, objection-aware nudge right after a webinar or info-call is the single biggest lever on enrollment — and the 'enroll your own home, earn it back' framing removes the financial risk.

emails.purpose: Convert the warm prospect — restate the opportunity, dismantle the cost risk with 'earn back your $99,' and hand them the referral link to enroll now.
emails.subject: About that $99 — here's how most Advisors earn it back in month one
emails.preview: You don't have to sell a thing to start. Switch your own home first.
keyPoints: Lead with risk reversal: enroll your own home and the bill offset can earn back the $99 membership in your first month (typical-results disclosure)
Pre-empt 'is this a pyramid scheme?' — you earn on real households switching to clean electricity, no inventory, no quotas, no monthly fee
One clear CTA: enroll today through your advisor's referral link
Light, honest urgency (founding-community / current bonus framing) — no false scarcity or earnings guarantees

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture (Rate-Quote & Opportunity Leads)
campaigns.objective: Move cold leads (rate-quote requesters, opportunity-curious) toward enrollment over a ~50-day cycle while defusing the MLM-credibility objection.
campaigns.trigger: Rate-quote tool submission (zip code) or opportunity info request; lead below the sales-ready score threshold.
campaigns.segment: Unconverted leads; branch by intent — customer-leaning (wants clean energy / savings) vs. advisor-leaning (wants income).
campaigns.rationale: The #1 conversion blocker is MLM skepticism, amplified by the founder's history; in this trust-driven channel a nurture that leads with transparency and proof is what earns the credibility to convert.

emails.purpose: Tackle the MLM / credibility objection head-on with transparency and proof, reframing Think+ as a legitimate clean-energy referral model.
emails.subject: "Isn't this just an MLM?" — a straight answer
emails.preview: No inventory, no monthly fees, real electricity customers. Here's exactly how it works.
emails.cta: See how Advisors actually get paid
keyPoints: Name the objection directly and answer with transparency — you refer real households to a licensed clean-electricity provider, not stockpile product
Differentiate from MLM stigma: $99/year, no monthly fee, no inventory, no quotas; residuals only while referred customers stay on plan
Proof: community/clean-energy impact plus a real advisor story, with FTC typical-results disclosure and no income guarantees
Low-friction next step: run a no-obligation rate quote or book a 15-minute opportunity call

campaigns.name: Advisor Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate Energy Advisors who have stalled — no referral in 60–90 days — before their annual membership lapses.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold: no referral and/or no Web Office login in 60–90 days.
campaigns.segment: Dormant advisors; branch by never-referred vs. lapsed-active (previously earning, now quiet).
campaigns.rationale: With ~70–80% year-one MLM attrition, most advisors stall after exhausting their inner circle; a focused win-back recovers 5–15% and protects renewal revenue before the $99 lapses.

emails.purpose: Reconnect with the stalled advisor, dissolve the 'I ran out of people to ask' wall, and hand them one concrete next referral move.
emails.subject: Still sitting on your $99, [First name]?
emails.preview: You're one referral away from proof this works. Here's the easiest one.
keyPoints: Empathize with the stall ('ran out of friends to ask') — almost every advisor hits this; it isn't the end of the road
Give one easy, specific move: a community-solar referral (higher bonus) or share the rate-quote tool with a neighbor
Remind them the membership is still active and what they'd forfeit if it lapses
Offer real human help (reply or book a coaching call); typical-results disclosure on any earning reference

campaigns.name: Renewal, Anniversary & Rank Expansion
campaigns.objective: Protect the annual $99 membership renewal and drive rank advancement and community-solar expansion among active advisors.
campaigns.trigger: Membership renewal date − 90 days; rank-advancement readiness signal (referral / downline growth).
campaigns.segment: Active advisors approaching renewal; branch by health — high-referral (expansion offer) vs. low (save / re-onboard).
campaigns.rationale: The annual renewal is the recurring-revenue backbone; quantifying the residuals and clean-energy impact an advisor earned this year is the strongest case to renew and to nudge them toward the next rank or solar upsell.

emails.purpose: Quantify the advisor's past-year results — referrals, residuals, clean-energy impact — to justify the renewal and tee up the next rank.
emails.subject: Your year with Think+, [First name] — by the numbers
emails.preview: The customers you switched, the residuals you earned, and what's one rank away.
keyPoints: Show their real numbers: customers referred, residuals earned, and clean-energy/community impact — pulled from actual data, not estimates
Tie renewal to continuity — residuals keep paying only while the membership stays active
Expansion hook: how close they are to the next of 8 ranks, plus the community-solar upsell ($60 vs. $20 bonus)
FTC typical-results disclosure; renew CTA plus a rank-coaching call offer for low-health accounts
